questions.text: The branch of physics that studies sound is ___________.
questions.explanation: Acoustics is the interdisciplinary science that deals with the study of all mechanical waves in gases, liquids, and solids including vibration, sound, ultrasound, and infrasound.

questions.text: Sound pressure levels (loudness or volume) are measured in ___________.
questions.explanation: The decibel (dB) is a logarithmic unit used to express the ratio of two values of a physical quantity, often power or intensity; in acoustics, it is used to measure sound pressure levels.

questions.text: The process of adjusting the level of a number of tracks to bring them all up to about the same level is called ___________.
questions.explanation: Normalization is the process of increasing (or decreasing) the amplitude of an entire audio signal so that the peak level matches a target (usually 0 dB or a specific standard level).

questions.text: When audio is measured to be digitally stored, the value of each measurement is rounded off to the nearest integer in a process called ___________.
questions.explanation: Quantization is the process of mapping continuous infinite values to a smaller set of discrete finite values, such as rounding analog voltage levels to the nearest digital integer.

questions.text: Reducing the number of separate measurements of an audio file is called ___________.
questions.explanation: Downsampling (or resampling to a lower rate) reduces the number of samples (measurements) per second in a digital audio file.

questions.text: The standard file format for displaying digitized motion video on the Macintosh is ___________.
questions.explanation: QuickTime (.mov) is the multimedia framework and file format developed by Apple Inc. for the Macintosh platform.

questions.text: The most common file format for editing sound on the Macintosh is ___________.
questions.explanation: AIFF (Audio Interchange File Format) was developed by Apple and is the standard uncompressed audio format for Mac computers.

questions.text: The audio file format introduced by Microsoft and IBM with the introduction of Windows is the ___________.
questions.explanation: WAV (Waveform Audio File Format) is the standard audio container format used on Windows PCs, developed by Microsoft and IBM.

questions.text: The process of playing a sound file while part of the file is still downloading is called ___________.
questions.explanation: Streaming allows media to be played in real-time as data is received over the network, rather than waiting for the entire file to download.

questions.text: Some software allows you to begin playing a downloading sound file as soon as enough of the sound is cached in your computer's ___________.
questions.explanation: A buffer is a temporary storage area in memory used to hold data while it is being transferred, allowing for smooth playback despite fluctuations in download speed.

questions.text: The file format that uses a shorthand representation of musical notes and durations stored in numeric form is:
questions.explanation: MIDI (Musical Instrument Digital Interface) stores performance data (notes, velocity, duration) rather than actual sound waveforms.

questions.text: Which of these statements regarding the MIDI audio format is **not** true?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The sound can easily be changed by changing instruments.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Spoken audio can easily be included.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Sound tracks can be created using sequencing software.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Files are generally smaller than the same digital audio sound.

questions.explanation: MIDI does not record actual sound (waveforms), so it cannot capture spoken audio or complex vocals unless they are triggered as samples.

questions.text: The primary benefit of the General MIDI over the previous MIDI specification is that:

choices.label: A
choices.text: the file sizes are much smaller due to the compression scheme

choices.label: B
choices.text: users can easily edit and adjust the data structures

choices.label: C
choices.text: it can be easily converted into the CD-ROM/XA format

choices.label: D
choices.text: the instruments are the same regardless of the playback source.

questions.explanation: General MIDI (GM) standardized the instrument patch map, ensuring that a file composed for a 'Piano' on one device would play back as a 'Piano' on any other GM-compliant device.

questions.text: What happens when an audio signal exceeds the recording device's maximum recording level?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The signal is compressed to an appropriate level.

choices.label: B
choices.text: \"Clipping\" of the signal occurs, introducing distortion.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The audio clip is extended to accommodate the extra data.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The extra bits go into a buffer for later use.

questions.explanation: Clipping occurs when an amplifier or recording device is pushed beyond its maximum capacity, resulting in the 'clipping' of waveform peaks and audible distortion.

questions.text: As one story goes, the criterion used to set the length of the sectors and ultimately the physical size of the compact disc format was based on the length of:
questions.explanation: Legend has it that the 74-minute capacity of a standard CD was chosen so it could hold a complete recording of Beethoven's Ninth Symphony.

questions.text: The process of recording a sound, stored in the form of thousands of individual measurements, each at a discrete point in time, is called:
questions.explanation: Sampling is the reduction of a continuous-time signal to a discrete-time signal by taking periodic measurements.

questions.text: The file size of a 5-second recording sampled at 22 kHz, 16-bit stereo (two tracks) would be about:
questions.explanation: Calculation: 22,000 samples/sec \* 2 bytes (16-bit) \* 2 channels (stereo) \* 5 seconds = 440,000 bytes.

questions.text: Which of the following sound file characteristics does **not** directly affect the size of a digital audio file?
questions.explanation: Volume (amplitude) is a value stored within the bits, but changing it does not change the amount of data (bits or samples) required to represent the audio.

questions.text: Each individual measurement of a sound that is stored as digital information is called a:
questions.explanation: A sample is the value or set of values at a point in time and/or space that represents the signal level.

questions.text: Audio recorded at 44.1 kHz (kilohertz), 16-bit stereo is considered:
questions.explanation: 44.1 kHz, 16-bit stereo is the standard audio specification for Compact Discs (CD-DA).

questions.text: Removing blank space or \"dead air\" at the beginning or end of a recording is sometimes called:
questions.explanation: Trimming refers to cutting the silent or unwanted portions at the start and end of an audio file.

questions.text: DSP stands for:
questions.explanation: Digital Signal Processing (DSP) is the use of digital processing, such as by computers or specialized digital signal processors, to perform a wide variety of signal processing operations.

questions.text: Sequencing software:

choices.label: A
choices.text: places audio clips in order in a sound track

choices.label: C
choices.text: applies filters to digital audio clips

choices.label: D
choices.text: manages a project by creating a timeline of events

questions.explanation: A sequencer is a device or application software that can record, edit, or play back music by handling MIDI data.

questions.text: The slower a user's connection, the longer he or she must wait for enough of the sound to download so that the entire file will have downloaded by the time the sound reaches the end. This effect is called:
questions.explanation: In the context of streaming, latency refers to the delay before the sound starts playing while the buffer is filled.

questions.text: The Red Book standard was so named because:

choices.label: A
choices.text: the standard was pioneered in the former Soviet Union

choices.label: B
choices.text: red is an acronym for \"Registered Electronic Data\"

choices.label: C
choices.text: the standard's book jacket was red

choices.label: D
choices.text: it was so expensive that producers were \"in the red\"

questions.explanation: The various standards for Compact Discs were published in books of different colors; the audio standard (CD-DA) was contained in the book with a red cover.
